name: Reproduction in Animals
subject: biology
summary: Sexual vs asexual reproduction. Male and female reproductive systems. Fertilisation, embryo development, and metamorphosis.

crushed.title: Sexual = two parents
crushed.text: Sperm (male) + Egg (female) fuse → zygote → embryo → baby. More variation.

crushed.title: Asexual = one parent
crushed.text: No gametes. Budding (hydra), binary fission (amoeba). Identical offspring.

crushed.title: Fertilisation types
crushed.text: Internal (inside body — humans, birds) vs External (outside — frogs, fish in water).

crushed.title: Metamorphosis
crushed.text: Larva looks totally different from adult. Tadpole → frog. Caterpillar → butterfly.

crushed.title: Oviparous vs viviparous
crushed.text: Oviparous = lay eggs (birds, frogs). Viviparous = give birth to young (humans, cows).

items.label: Sexual reproduction
items.detail: Two parents, gametes fuse, genetic variation in offspring

items.label: Asexual — budding
items.detail: Hydra grows a bud that detaches as new individual

items.label: Asexual — fission
items.detail: Amoeba splits into two identical cells

items.label: Internal fertilisation
items.detail: Fusion inside female body — humans, reptiles, birds

items.label: External fertilisation
items.detail: Eggs fertilised outside body in water — frogs, fish

flows.title: Human Reproduction — From Fertilisation to Birth
flows.description: Follow the journey from sperm meeting egg to a developing baby.

steps.label: Fertilisation
steps.description: Fusion in fallopian tube forms a single-celled zygote.

steps.label: Zygote
steps.description: Zygote divides repeatedly while moving to the uterus.

steps.label: Embryo
steps.description: Ball of cells embeds in uterus wall. Gets nourishment from mother.

steps.label: Foetus
steps.description: After 8 weeks, called foetus — heart, brain, limbs form.

steps.label: Birth
steps.description: Uterus contracts — baby born after gestation period.

flows.title: Frog Metamorphosis
flows.description: Tadpole transforms completely into an adult frog.

steps.label: Eggs
steps.description: Female frog lays eggs in water. Male fertilises externally.

steps.label: Tadpole
steps.description: Aquatic larva — breathes with gills, has tail, no legs.

steps.label: Changes
steps.description: Hind legs appear first, then front legs. Tail absorbed.

steps.label: Lungs develop
steps.description: Tadpole starts breathing air with developing lungs.

steps.label: Adult frog
steps.description: Fully transformed frog — lives on land, returns to water to breed.

keyTerms.analogy: Half an instruction manual from each parent

keyTerms.definition: Fertilised egg — first cell of new organism
Transformation from larva to adult with different body form
keyTerms.analogy: A complete makeover between life stages
The waiting period before the big arrival

demos.title: Internal Fertilisation — Fallopian Tube
demos.description: Watch an egg release from the ovary, sperm swim through the fallopian tube, and fusion form a zygote.

steps.label: Egg released
steps.description: Ovary releases a mature egg into the fallopian tube (oviduct).

steps.label: Sperm reach egg
steps.description: Sperm swim up the tube; one penetrates the egg membrane.

steps.label: Zygote forms
steps.description: Sperm and egg nuclei fuse — a single-celled zygote begins dividing.

chapterSummary.overview: Reproduction creates new individuals and continues the species. This chapter covers sexual reproduction (fusion of sperm and egg), asexual reproduction (budding, fission), internal vs external fertilisation, embryo development, metamorphosis, and the difference between egg-laying and live-bearing animals.
keyPoints: Sexual reproduction involves two parents and gametes (sperm + egg) → zygote → embryo.
Asexual reproduction: one parent, no gametes — budding (Hydra), binary fission (Amoeba).
Internal fertilisation (humans, birds) vs external fertilisation (frogs, fish in water).
Oviparous animals lay eggs; viviparous animals give birth to live young.
Metamorphosis: larva transforms into adult (tadpole → frog, caterpillar → butterfly).
chapterSummary.examFocus: Compare sexual vs asexual reproduction. Draw the path of sperm to egg. Explain metamorphosis with examples.
